_comment: M&A / Data-room Overview (buyer-facing) — Northwind Holdings (DEMO). All figures synthetic and ILLUSTRATIVE, not real Tiny data.
title: Project North — Business Overview

slides.title: Business Overview
slides.subtitle: Buyer overview and data-room summary for a controlled process.

slides.title: Business at a Glance
slides.lead: Northwind is a profitable, founder-owned holding company of six software and consumer brands.
bullets: What we are. A diversified group of six brands — Acme, Initech, Umbrella, Globex, Hooli and Stark — spanning B2B SaaS, creative tools and consumer hardware.
Model. Majority subscription and recurring revenue with a high-margin transactional layer; centralized finance, infrastructure and M&A, with autonomous brand teams.
Scale. ~$156M revenue and ~$31M Adjusted EBITDA (27% margin) on an LTM basis, generated across ~40 countries.
Moats. Entrenched brands, low customer concentration, ~115% net revenue retention in the SaaS segment, and proprietary IP across each business.
People. ~420 full-time team members; experienced, retained brand leadership with no single-person dependency.
slides.footnote: Illustrative demo data — Northwind Holdings (DEMO) is synthetic and not a real entity.

slides.title: Financial Profile
commentary: Durable top line. ~$156M LTM revenue growing low-double-digits organically, with no single brand above ~30% of the total.
High-quality earnings. ~$31M normalized Adjusted EBITDA at a 27% margin, with strong cash conversion and low maintenance capex.
Diversified mix. Recurring SaaS and subscriptions anchor the model; transactional and hardware add upside without concentration risk.
slides.footnote: Illustrative figures for a synthetic demo company. Adj. EBITDA is normalized — see Normalized EBITDA bridge.

slides.title: Recurring vs. Transactional
commentary: ~72% recurring. Recurring SaaS and subscriptions make up roughly $113M of $156M LTM revenue.
Predictable base. ~115% net revenue retention in SaaS underpins forward visibility; transactional and hardware add high-margin upside.
slides.note: Illustrative revenue split ($M, LTM). Demo data.

slides.title: Normalized EBITDA
slides.note: ILLUSTRATIVE quality-of-earnings bridge — synthetic demo data, subject to buyer QoE confirmation.

slides.title: What's Included
slides.lead: The transaction conveys the full operating group and its supporting assets.
bullets: Operating brands. 100% of Acme, Initech, Umbrella, Globex, Hooli and Stark — all customers, revenue and contracts.
Intellectual property. Source code, trademarks, domains, patents and design assets across all six brands.
Team. ~420 employees including retained brand leadership; key-person agreements and standard transition support.
Contracts. Customer agreements, key vendor and reseller relationships, and assignable cloud / infrastructure commitments.
Infrastructure. Shared finance, data and engineering platforms; data-room contains org charts, financials and material contracts.
Excluded. Surplus cash, the founder's personal vehicles and two non-core minority investments are excluded from scope.
slides.footnote: Indicative scope — final perimeter to be confirmed in definitive agreements. Demo data.

slides.title: Transaction Considerations
slides.lead: A clean, well-prepared process designed to move quickly with the right partner.
bullets: Structure. 100% equity sale (or asset purchase) on a cash-free, debt-free basis with a normalized working-capital target.
Valuation basis. Marketed on normalized Adj. EBITDA of ~$31M; illustrative range subject to diligence and final structure.
Process & timeline. IOIs requested within ~3 weeks, management meetings and confirmatory diligence over ~6 weeks, signing targeted within ~90 days.
Diligence. Populated data room covering financials, QoE, legal, tax, HR and IP; third-party QoE and tech reviews supported.
Next steps. Sign NDA, access the data room, submit a non-binding IOI, and schedule management presentations.
slides.footnote: Illustrative terms for a synthetic demo. Not an offer or solicitation.

slides.subtitle: Confidential buyer overview — questions and next steps welcome.
